title: Clinopodium × alboviride
taxonomy: Clinopodium × alboviride is a hybrid of the plants Clinopodium glabrum and Clinopodium vulgare. It belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This hybrid is native to Central Europe. Clinopodium × alboviride is a perennial herb, and its classification is further classified into the subfamily Nepetoideae. It is a member of the genus Clinopodium, which consists of various species of flowering plants.
benefits: Clinopodium × alboviride has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. It is valued for its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and infections. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including essential oils and phenolic acids, that contribute to its medicinal properties. Studies suggest that Clinopodium × alboviride may have a positive effect on gut health and immune system function. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential uses of this plant.

It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and respiratory issues. constituents: Clinopodium × alboviride contains a variety of bioactive compounds including essential oils and phenolic acids. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The essential oil of Clinopodium × alboviride contains mainly monoterpene and sesquiterpene compounds, which contribute to its aroma and therapeutic effects. Phenolic acids, particularly rosmarinic acid, have been identified as key components of the plant's bioactive profile. preparations: Clinopodium × alboviride is known to be used in herbal preparations, primarily due to its carminative and antispasmodic properties. The plant's extracts are often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps. In traditional medicine, herbal preparations made from Clinopodium × alboviride are used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea. intro: Clinopodium × alboviride, also known as white-hairy calamint, is a hybrid plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is commonly found in Eastern Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. The bioactive constituents of Clinopodium × alboviride include volatile oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the leaves of Clinopodium × alboviride are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
